supporting_works.title: The magnetic and botanic family physician, and domestic practice of natural medicine : with illustrations showing various phases of mesmeric treatment, including full and concise instruction in mesmerism, curative magnetism, massage, and medical botany / by D. Younger.

The Cherokee physician, or, Indian guide to health : comprising a brief view of anatomy, with general rules for preserving health without the use of medicines : the diseases of the United States, with their symptoms, causes, and means of prevention are explained in simple terms : it also contains a description of a variety of herbs and roots, many of which are not explained in any other book, the medicinal virtues of which have hitherto been unknown to the whites
supporting_works.creator: Foreman, Richard, active 1849-1857.

supporting_works.title: Our family physician : a plain, practical, and reliable guide to the detection and treatment of all the diseases common to this country : designed for use by people who have not made the practice of medicine a study or business, and who are not supposed to be familiar with technical phrases and names, but want information stated in plain English : gives, separately, with every disease, the treatment prescribed by each of the "schools" and full and explicit directions for nursing the sick, preparation of food for the sick, etc

overview.title: Medical companion, treating according to the most successful practice, of the diseases to which man is subject : with a description of vegetable medicines, and the manner of preparing and using them : also, a description of roots and herbs : to which is added an essay on hygia, or the art of preserving health and prolonging life
overview.excerpt: Slippery elm bark taken constantly, in decoction, has been found very effectual in the cure of coughs, erup- tions, whites, gleets, ulcers, &c. and is one of the best poultices for fresh wound,s burns, scalds, bruises...

retrieval_text: Slippery Elm is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 133 works and 447 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Slippery Elm. Common plant-part co-mentions include Bark, Root, Leaf, Seed, Gum, Flower. Common preparation co-mentions include Poultice, Tea, Powder, Oil, Infusion, Decoction. Common condition co-mentions include Inflammation, Wounds or ulcers, Cold or catarrh, Fever, Cough, Consumption. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Allergy or sensitivity caution, Toxicity watch, Narcotic or sedative caution, Child-use caution, Fatal-risk warning. Representative source works include The American practice abridged, or The family physician : being the scientific system of medicine; The American practice condensed, or The family physician : being the scientific system of medicine; The American practice condensed, or The family physician : being the scientific system of medicine; The family physician ; or, The reformed practice of medicine : on vegetable or botanical principles : being a compendium of the "American practice". This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
